access.user: Can read exam information. Cannot modify exam fact data.
access.admin: Maintains exam data through admin or import flows, not direct edits on ordinary pages.
access.agent: After authorization, can read exams relevant to the current user; this capability is primarily exposed via MCP.

rules.attached-to-section: Exams are attached to sections and are high-priority time events.
rules.read-only: Exams are read-only; no ordinary user edit entry point is provided.
rules.semester-required: Semesters must be explicitly displayed when browsing exams across semesters.
rules.mobile-toolbar-priority: At mobile widths, completion-status filters stay directly available, card/list display mode moves into an accessible overflow menu, and toolbar controls remain a minimum 44 by 44 CSS pixel target. Desktop toolbar behavior remains unchanged.

fields.name: exams
notes: DateTime range inputs normalize to Asia/Shanghai calendar dates; unknown-date exams remain included by default.
Each Exam.examRooms(page: PageInput) field returns ExamRoomPage! with the shared default page 1/pageSize 20 and maximum pageSize 100.

tools.name: list_my_exams
notes: Returns exams[]; each item contains section.course.namePrimary, section.code, section.semester?.nameCn, examDate, startTime, endTime, examBatch, and examRooms[].
Omitting semesterId reads all followed semesters; pass a semesterId to recover one current or past term.

fields: mobile compact toolbar with direct completion filters and overflow display-mode control

tools.name: list_exams_by_section
tools.returns: { found: Boolean, section: Section?, exams: ExamEntry[] }
